evidence_quotes.quote: paying for loot boxes, upgrade packs etc.

items.topic: End Of Support In Video Games

evidence_quotes.quote: End of support in video games.

paragraphs.text: Hello there, I would like to give my thought/feedback on this issue. My main issues as as followed: End of support in video games. Misleading advertisement. Pay to win transactions. End of support in video games. I had bought a games to play with my friends (Dark souls 2), and the servers were taken down on March 31st 2024 which meant, I was not able to play online any more, nor is ti possible to create custom servers. The only other way I could still play this game, was to purchase an upgrade basically of for 25 euros. which was not really an option for me back then. And considering I had already bought the game I would like to still be able to enjoy it with my friends. Misleading advertisement. This is for mostly all the mobile game ads we see every day, BUT this also has to do with new game releases, where the people are given a rendering of the game as a trailer, explaining who the game works and showing the amazing graphics that the teams managed to create, or how different mechanics work, only to buy a game, and even with the graphics settings turn to the highest point, the game lacks the liveliness that was promised, and the mechanics are lacking crucial key things features/details, that prevent them from being as promised. As an excample for this I will mention the game: Middle-Earth Shadow of War "the difference between the marketing/trailer presentation and the real gameplay experience, especially around fortress battles, led to community disappointment. But only then over time, updates improved parts of the system, but many players still felt the trailers over promised on what was ultimately a less dynamic fortress feature." And finally as of pay to win. It's understandable that not everyone wants to spend time leveling up characters, or searching to find items, etc. But when you add the option to pay money to get and guarantee most of the Items that take quite a few hour to obtain, someone can get then in 30 minutes paying for loot boxes, upgrade packs etc. it creates an incentive for users to spend more money by skipping all the progress, and everyone else that has to play against them have to deal with overpowered enemies, or even unfair advantages at some cases. I understand that I might not have explained everything as needed because I don't want to take a lot of your time, but I think that this will be enough. Thank you for the chance to voice my opinion, and on your time!
